title: The Iron Giant
author: Brad Bird
snippet: Brad Bird's first feature, and probably the best science-fiction film for kids ever made. Superman-tested.
full_text:
In the small town of Rockwell, Maine in October 1957, a giant metal machine befriends a nine-year-old boy and ultimately finds its humanity by unselfishly saving people from their own fears and prejudices.

Watch on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store
watch_on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store

title: Kiki's Delivery Service
author: Hayao Miyazaki
snippet: Miyazaki's most kid-friendly Studio Ghibli — gentle, magical, no scary villain. A 5-year-old can sit through it.
full_text:
A young witch, on her mandatory year of independent life, finds fitting into a new community difficult while she supports herself by running an air courier service.

Watch on: HBO Max, HBO Max Amazon Channel
tagline: I was feeling blue, but I'm better now.
watch_on: HBO Max, HBO Max Amazon Channel

title: Toy Story
author: John Lasseter
snippet: If they haven't seen the original, they should. Holds up exactly thirty years later.
full_text:
Led by Woody, Andy's toys live happily in his room until Andy's birthday brings Buzz Lightyear onto the scene. Afraid of losing his place in Andy's heart, Woody plots against Buzz. But when circumstances separate Buzz and Woody from their owner, the duo eventually learns to put aside their differences.

Watch on: Disney Plus
tagline: The adventure takes off when toys come to life!

title: How to Train Your Dragon
author: Chris Sanders
snippet: Dragon-flying scenes still hit. Strong score, real emotional stakes, no juvenile humor crutches.
full_text:
As the son of a Viking leader on the cusp of manhood, shy Hiccup Horrendous Haddock III faces a rite of passage: he must kill a dragon to prove his warrior mettle. But after downing a feared dragon, he realizes that he no longer wants to destroy it, and instead befriends the beast – which he names Toothless – much to the chagrin of his warrior father.

Watch on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store
tagline: What started as fire and fury will become friendship.
watch_on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store

title: Paddington
author: Paul King
snippet: The friendliest film ever made. Adults cry, kids laugh, the bear is competent.
full_text:
A young Peruvian bear travels to London in search of a home. Finding himself lost and alone at Paddington Station, he meets the kindly Brown family, who offer him a temporary haven.

Watch on: Britbox Apple TV Channel , Angel Studios
tagline: Please look after this bear. Thank you.
watch_on: Britbox Apple TV Channel , Angel Studios

title: The Goonies
author: Richard Donner
snippet: Pirate treasure, secret tunnels, a bunch of kids with no parents in sight. Slightly age-up — better for 8–10.
full_text:
Young teen Mikey Walsh and his friends set off on a quest to find Pirate One-Eyed Willie's treasure in hopes of saving their homes from demolition. However, on their quest to find the treasure, they run into a family of recently escaped criminals, determined to capture the kids and reach the treasure first.

Watch on: fuboTV, Freeform
tagline: They call themselves "The Goonies." The secret caves. The old lighthouse. The lost map. The treacherous traps. The hidden adventure. And Sloth… Join the adventure.

title: Babe
author: Chris Noonan
snippet: A pig who herds sheep with kindness. Adults will be unprepared for how moving it still is.
full_text:
Babe is a little pig who doesn't quite know his place in the world. With a bunch of odd friends, like Ferdinand the duck who thinks he is a rooster and Fly the dog he calls mum, Babe realises that he has the makings to become the greatest sheep pig of all time, and Farmer Hoggett knows it. With the help of the sheep dogs, Babe learns that a pig can be anything that he wants to be.

Watch on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store
tagline: A little pig goes a long way.
watch_on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store

title: The Incredibles
author: Brad Bird
snippet: Pixar's only movie that works as a dad movie too. Fast pacing, no slow middle.
full_text:
Bob Parr has given up his superhero days to log in time as an insurance adjuster and raise his three children with his formerly heroic wife in suburbia. But when he receives a mysterious assignment, it's time to get back into costume.

Watch on: Disney Plus

title: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial
author: Steven Spielberg
snippet: Probably their first taste of a film that earns its tears. Spielberg at his most direct.
full_text:
An alien is left behind on Earth and saved by the 10-year-old Elliott who decides to keep him hidden in his home. While a task force hunts for the extra-terrestrial, Elliott, his brother, and his little sister Gertie form an emotional bond with their new friend, and try to help him find his way home.

Watch on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store
tagline: He is afraid. He is totally alone. He is 3,000,000 light years from home.
watch_on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store

title: Wallace & Gromit: The Curse of the Were-Rabbit
author: Steve Box
snippet: Stop-motion claymation at its sharpest. Inventive enough that adults stay awake.
full_text:
Cheese-loving eccentric Wallace and his cunning canine pal Gromit run a business ridding the town of garden pests. Using only humane methods, which turns their home into a halfway house for evicted vermin, the pair stumble upon a mystery involving a voracious vegetarian monster that threatens to ruin the annual veggie-growing contest.

Watch on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store
watch_on: Rent: Amazon Video, Apple TV Store
